repo_prior_art.cite: eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator: finite LP envelope over a bridge/coupling polytope; pid_mediation_proxy: failed full-fiber proxy-null interval

published_prior_art.cite: GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023ProxyPI: proxy partial-identification bounds without completeness

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy: rejected precisely because finite realization of every proxy-null array was missing

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997JASA gives finite IV response-type sharpness, not negative-control bridge-envelope realization

repo_prior_art.cite: q1_spectral_phase_transition: singular-value phase idea in panel/FWL algebra, not proximal bridge identified sets

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023SPL, BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT, and Santos2011JoE already cover functional identification despite nonunique nuisances

novelty_flags.label: proposal-tier
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: after Conjecture 1, Conjecture 2 is mostly the deterministic width trichotomy of a sharp interval, so the proposal needs a stronger generic-class realization or nontrivial phase theorem to reach flagship.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Upgrade Conjecture 1 from rational finite-law realization to an explicit generic-class response-type construction with a support-size bound and a non-degenerate worked example.

Split Conjecture 2 into a theorem conditional on sharpness for the tautological point/interval/Manski trichotomy, and isolate the residual-tolerance bound as the genuinely new mathematical statement with a sharp constant or lower-bound example.
Add a compact comparison table separating Ghassami proxy bounds, Zhang/Bennett/Santos functional identification, and the proposed finite bridge-envelope realization step.
Add finite examples exhibiting all three phases with explicit endpoint LP certificates, including a nontrivial interval case that is neither point identified nor Manski-trivial.

recommended_next_step: Revise toward a flagship kernel by making the realization theorem and residual-tolerance phase boundary do non-tautological work beyond the sharp-interval LP definition.
